review_reason: Phase 4 (Irreflexivity Proof) is blocked: the conservative extension + lifting theorem resolves F+ to F derivation transfer but does not resolve the semantic gap in the Goldblatt/BdRV naming argument. The F-shadow M' of the F+-MCS M'_ext equals M itself when CanonicalR(M,M), so the naming argument doesn't yield a genuinely new F-MCS for the contradiction. Three possible resolution paths identified: (1) replicate F+-MCS machinery to derive contradiction within F+, (2) product frame bypass, (3) revised proof structure.

partial_progress.details: Phases 1-3 complete (sorry-free). Phase 4 blocked: conservative extension resolves syntactic transfer but not the irreflexivity proof's semantic gap. The standard naming argument requires M subset M' (global freshness), which the F+ embedding provides. But the contradiction requires neg(q) in M'_ext, which contradicts q in M'_ext. Analysis shows M' (F-shadow of M'_ext) = M when CanonicalR(M,M).

errors.message: Phase 4 blocked: naming argument's final contradiction step requires neg(q) in M'_ext, but q in M'_ext prevents this. The F-shadow M' = M when CanonicalR(M,M), so GContent/HContent duality yields no new information.
errors.recommendation: Consider: (1) replicate F+-MCS machinery for full F+ naming argument, (2) product frame approach from IRRSoundness.lean, (3) fundamentally different proof structure

completion_data.completion_summary: Proved lift_derivation_qfree (conservative extension theorem) establishing that F+ is a conservative extension of F: any F+ derivation of embedded F-formulas projects back to an F derivation. Phase 4 (irreflexivity proof) is blocked due to a genuine mathematical gap in translating the naming argument's contradiction step.
